question: Define a C++ struct named `Book` with members `title` (std::string), `author` (std::string), and `publication_year` (int). Create an array of three `Book` objects, prompt the user to enter details for each, and then print the details of all books.

test_cases.input:
The Great Gatsby
F. Scott Fitzgerald
2000
Nineteen Eighty-Four
George Orwell
1984
The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Douglas Adams
2005
test_cases.expected:
Enter details for three books:

Book 1:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year:

Book 2:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year:

Book 3:
Enter title: Enter author: Enter publication year:

--- Book Details ---

Book 1:
Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Publication Year: 2000

Book 2:
Title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
Author: George Orwell
Publication Year: 1984

Book 3:
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
Publication Year: 2005

errors: Excessive use of std::endl instead of '\n' which can reduce efficiency due to frequent buffer flushes.
Uses a C-style array (Book books[3]) instead of a more modern and safer container like std::array or std::vector for a fixed-size collection.
